title: VaultBackend::put returns Backend error for permanent config issue
description: When ?field= is absent, put returns SecretError::Backend { backend: 'vault', ... }. Backend semantically means 'the vault server returned an error; retry later'. A caller doing match err { SecretError::Backend { .. } => retry_in(5s) } will loop forever on a configuration mistake. The correct variant would be InvalidUri (this is a URI misconfiguration) but that variant is defined as 'syntactically invalid URI', not 'URI insufficient for this operation'. Suggested: check self.field.is_none() in from_uri only when a write mode can be inferred — or add an Unsupported variant to SecretError. Short-term: use DecodeFailed with a clear message, which at least signals 'not a transient error'.
